def: v.t. = doshnā, q.v.

ety: Pehl. dośt; Zend zusta, perf. part. fr. zush; = S. जुष्ट, rt. जुष्
def: s.m. A friend, one beloved; a lover, a sweetheart
subs: vulg. dos-dār, s.m. A friend, &c. ( = dost); — adj. & adv. Friendly; lovingly
vulg. dos-dārī, s.f. Friendliness, friendship; love, affection
adj. & s.m. Appearing friendly, in the guise of a friend; — a seeming friend
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دوسْت (dōst), from Middle Persian 𐭣𐭥𐭮𐭲𐭩 (dwst' /⁠dōst⁠/), from Old Persian 𐎭𐎢𐏁𐎫𐎠 (d-u-š-t-a /⁠dauštā⁠⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *jawštā́, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ȷ́awštā́, from Proto-Indo-European *ǵéws-tōr ~ *ǵus-tr-és, from *ǵews-.

ety: dost, q.v.+Zend suff. ānā, or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Friendly, kind; amicably, affectionately
subs: adv. In a friendly way, in friendship, as a friend.

ety: dost, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Amity, friendship; love, affection, attachment, intimacy; illicit love
subs: (kī-), To make a boast of friendship (for), to pretend or affect friendship (for)
(-se), To form a friendship or an intimacy (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دوسْتِی (dōstī). By surface analysis, دوسْت (dost) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. Land held free in the name of Hindū deities (ostensibly for the provision of all the necessaries of divine worship).
part. adj. & s.m. Piercing, transfixing; sewing; — one who sews (used in comp. e.g. ḵẖaima-doz, 'a tent-maker'; zar-doz, 'embroiderer').
s.m. A plant the fruit of which is a prickly nut (of the size of the filbert), and which adheres to a garment coming in contact; — gum lac (used in fastening the handles of knives and swords).

ety: old P. duzhakh; Pehl. dushaqv; Zend duzhaṅh, fr. duz?; S. दुह्
def: s.m. Hell; — (met.) the belly
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دوزَخ (dōzax). First attested as Old Hindi दोजग (dojaga).

ety: dozaḵẖ, q.v.+ī = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. & s.m. Hellish, infernal, damned; hell-deserving; the people of hell, the damned; a dweller in hell; a great sinner; — (met.) a glutton.
s.f. Piercing, pricking; sewing (used in comp. e.g. ḵẖaima-dozī).
s.m. Substance, matter, thing, object
subs: Moneyless, having no property or effects, poor, indigent.

def: corr. of draviṛ, and drāviḍ, qq.v.
s.m. Length; a degree of longitude, longitude.
s.f. = dākh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Containing a droṇ; sown with a droṇ of grain (a field); — a field sown with a droṇ of grain.
s.f. A vessel holding a droṇ by measure.
s.m. Running, flowing; fusing, liquefaction
subs: s.m. A kind of borax, a flux.

def: adj. Flowing; fluid; liquid.
adj. Causing to run or flow, diuretic; solvent, discutient.
s.m. See s.v. drāva.
s.m. Fusing; — the fruit of Strychnos potatorum, used for purifying water; the clearing nut.
s.m. (old H.) = dravya, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Draviḍian, belonging to the Drāviḍas; — a Drāviḍa; — the Drāviḍ people and their country (properly the coast of Coromandel from Madras to Cape Comorin, or the country in which Tamul is spoken; in a more general sense, the name is applied to four other countries, viz. Karnāṭa, Gujjarāt, Mahārāshtra, and Telinga); a Brāhman of Drāviḍa, or of the south.
s.f. A Draviḍian female; — a cardamom.
s.f. Saliva.
s.m. A valuable possession, property, &c. ( = dravya, q.v.); strength, power, vigour
subs: s.m. 'Destroying vigour'; the plant Hyperanthera moringa.

def: = drāviḍ, q.v.
s.m. Name of a district and people on the east coast of the Deccan (dakkhan; — the people are regarded as degraded Kshatriyas); a man of that race.
s.m. = drāviṛak, q.v.
s.m. Zedoary, Curcuma zedoaria; — black salt.
s.f. Name of a rāginī, or musical mode.

ety: drav˚ = S. द्रव(ति), rt. द्रु+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To run, flow, be fluid or liquid, to melt, dissolve, rarify; (met.) to melt, to feel compassion, be moved with pity, to exercise mercy.

s.m. A substance, thing, object; the ingredients or materials of anything; matter; elementary substance (of which nine kinds are reckoned in the Nyāya philosophy, viz., earth, water, fire, air, ether, time, space, soul, and mind); — an object possessed, a possession, wealth, property, goods, money; — bell-metal, brass; — a drug, medicament
subs: (˚ya+ar˚), s.m. Acquiring property, gain or acquisition of wealth
s.m. Accumulation of property or wealth, store of worldly goods
or -vat, or -vant, adj. (f. vatī), Having property, possessed of substance, rich, wealthy.

def: s.m. The third part of a sign of the zodiac; the demi-god presiding over that part (the Decanus of European astrology).

adj. (f. -ā), Fixed, firm, hard, strong, solid, massive; firmly fastened, shut; tough; stiff; compact; tight, close, dense; thick, coarse; able, powerful; steady, persevering; reliable; durable, confirmed, established; secure; severe, violent; (in Math.) reduced to the last term or smallest number by a common divisor; — adv. Firmly, strongly, tightly, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Ascetic; orthodox; — an ascetic, &c.
(˚ḍha+an˚), adj. Firm-bodied, strong, hard, stout, stalwart; — s.m. A diamond
adj. & s.m. Strong in battle; — a hero
s.f. 'Winding closely round'; a species of creeper, Echites frutescens
dr̤iṛh-bhakt, adj. 'Firm in devotion,' faithful, devoted
s.m. 'Having a hard fruit'; the cocoa-nut
adj. & s.m. Firm or resolute of mind, staunch-minded; — a resolute or staunch-minded person; — resoluteness, staunch-mindedness
v.n. To remain or stand firm, to maintain (one's) ground; to hold out, insist, persist
s.f. 'Having strong fibres'; a plant from the fibres of which bow-strings are made, Sanseviera zeylanica
adj. & s.m. Firm in friendship, constant; — a firm friend
s.m. 'Having a strong stem'; a bamboo
adj. & s.m. Having coarse hair; — coarse hair; bristles; — a wild hog
adj. & s.m. Close-fisted, tight-fisted; niggardly, miserly; — one whose hand is difficult to open; a niggard, miser
adj. Having a fixed determination; certain, confirmed, corroborated, undoubted.

def: s.m. = dr̤ik, q.v.
s.m. (but f. in S.), The eye; sight, view; perception; wisdom
subs: s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), lit. 'Having eyes for ears'; a snake (which in the opinion of the Hindūs, has no visible external ear)
and dr̤in-manḍal, s.m. A small circle on the axis of the earth within the greater circles of the armillary sphere, accompanying each planetary circle or orbit.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Fixed, firm, hard, strong, solid, massive; firmly fastened, shut; tough; stiff; compact; tight, close, dense; thick, coarse; able, powerful; steady, persevering; reliable; durable, confirmed, established; secure; severe, violent; (in Math.) reduced to the last term or smallest number by a common divisor; — adv. Firmly, strongly, tightly, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Ascetic; orthodox; — an ascetic, &c.
(˚ḍha+an˚), adj. Firm-bodied, strong, hard, stout, stalwart; — s.m. A diamond
adj. & s.m. Strong in battle; — a hero
s.f. 'Winding closely round'; a species of creeper, Echites frutescens
dr̤iṛh-bhakt, adj. 'Firm in devotion,' faithful, devoted
s.m. 'Having a hard fruit'; the cocoa-nut
adj. & s.m. Firm or resolute of mind, staunch-minded; — a resolute or staunch-minded person; — resoluteness, staunch-mindedness
v.n. To remain or stand firm, to maintain (one's) ground; to hold out, insist, persist
s.f. 'Having strong fibres'; a plant from the fibres of which bow-strings are made, Sanseviera zeylanica
adj. & s.m. Firm in friendship, constant; — a firm friend
s.m. 'Having a strong stem'; a bamboo
adj. & s.m. Having coarse hair; — coarse hair; bristles; — a wild hog
adj. & s.m. Close-fisted, tight-fisted; niggardly, miserly; — one whose hand is difficult to open; a niggard, miser
adj. Having a fixed determination; certain, confirmed, corroborated, undoubted.

def: s.f. A faithful or devoted mistress or wife.

ety: dr̤iṛh+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता +इका and ताई = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Fixedness, firmness, hardness, solidity, strength; steadiness, perseverance; confirmation, corroboration.

ety: dr̤iṛh, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment)
def: v.t. To strengthen; to confirm, corroborate; to prove.

ety: dr̤iṛh+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता +इका and ताई = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Fixedness, firmness, hardness, solidity, strength; steadiness, perseverance; confirmation, corroboration.

ety: dr̤iṛh+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता +इका and ताई = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Fixedness, firmness, hardness, solidity, strength; steadiness, perseverance; confirmation, corroboration.
s.f. The eye
subs: (˚śā+up˚), s.m. 'Resembling the eye'; the white lotus, Nelumbrium speciosum.

def: s.f. = dr̤ishṭi, q.v.
part. Seen, looked at, beheld, perceived, observed; visible, apparent, observable; considered, regarded; experienced, suffered, endured
subs: (˚ṭa+an˚), s.m. 'The end or aim of what is seen, or what is in view,' example, precedent, comparison, illustration; parable, allegory, simile, type, trope
To give an example, to liken, to exemplify, to illustrate
or -vat, or -vant, adj. (f. vatī), Having seen or beheld; seeing, beholding.

def: s.m. See s.v. dr̤ishṭa.
s.f. Seeing, looking, at, viewing; the faculty of seeing, sight, vision, view; the eye; look; eye-sight; the pupil of the eye
subs: s.m. Mortgage or pledge of real property, without possession (but such as the mortgagee can keep in view); deed of hypothecation
s.m. 'Falling of the sight'; a look, glance
s.m. Influence of the malignant eye
s.m. 'Suitable to the faculty of seeing'; the plant Hibiscus mutabilis
To look, behold, look (at, or upon, -kī or -par), to take a view (of), cast the eyes (on, -par); to take notice (of), pay heed or attention (to)
s.m. Range or compass of the sight; — adj. Within range or compass of the sight, in view, visible, perceptible

def: s.f. The eye ( = dr̤iśā, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. dr̤iśā.
part. adj. To be seen, visible; worthy of being seen or looked at; beautiful, pleasing; — s.m. (in Arith.), A known or given quantity or number
subs: adj. Of the nature of visible water
adj. (f. -matī), Under the view, under survey or inspection.

def: s.f. Visibility; vision, sight.
s.m. Injury, insidious wounding, hostile action, mischief, malice, spite, enmity, hatred, rancour; perfidy, treachery; trespass, wrong, offence; rebellion
subs: s.f. Injurious design, malice prepense, the wish, thought, or attempt to injure
(-se), To act maliciously or spitefully (towards), to be inimical or hostile (to).

def: s.m. A species of metre, the dohā or stanza of Hindī poetry.
adj. & s.m. Hurting, harming, injuring, endeavouring to hurt, malicious, malignant, spiteful, inimical, hostile, mischievous; — a spiteful or a mischievous person, an enemy, a malignant.
adj. & s.m. Hurting, harming, injuring, endeavouring to hurt, malicious, malignant, spiteful, inimical, hostile, mischievous; — a spiteful or a mischievous person, an enemy, a malignant.
s.f. A kind of shrub.

s.m. A wooden tub, a trough; a certain measure of capacity ( = one āḍhak, and also = four āḍhaks); — the sixteenth part of a kharī, or forty-eight gallons; — a weight of thirty-six seers (about sixty-four pounds avoirdupois); — a raven, a carrion-crow
subs: s.m. The capital of a district, the chief of 400 villages ( = droṇī-mukh).

def: s.f. A canoe, raft (see ḍongā), small boat made from the sheath of a plantain tree; any boat-shaped vessel; a wooden tub, a trough, a bucket; — a valley
s.m. A tree ( = drum)
subs: s.m. A species of Pinus devadāru.

def: s.m. See s.v. dru.
s.m. A tree of Svarga or paradise
subs: (˚ma+ām˚), s.m. 'Tree-disease'; lac, the animal dye, resin
(˚ma+īś˚), s.m. 'The king of trees,' the palm-tree
vulg. durm-indr (˚ma+in˚), s.m. King of the forest and the waters (syn. ḵẖẉāja ḵẖiẓr).

def: adj. Quick, speedy, swift; runaway, flown, escaped
subs: s.m. A quick pace or step; — adj. & adv. Quick of step, going quickly; — quickly.

ety: Pehl. du; Zend dva; S. dva
def: adj. Two
subs: do-āb, du-āba, s.m. A tract of country lying between two rivers which unite after running some distance; the country between the Ganges and Jamnā (called the antar-bed)
s.m. Double-distilled liquor, very strong wine, &c.
do-arthī, adj. & s.m. Double-meaning, ambiguous, equivocal; — double meaning, ambiguity; double-entendre
adj. & s.m. Having two horses; having one horse to relieve another; quick, expeditious; — a relay of two horses
s.m. lit. 'Having two rooms'; a kind of tent with two rooms
du-annī, s.f. A small silver coin, the eighth part of a rupee
adj. One or so, a few; — s.m. = du-sāla, and dok, qq.v.
adj. & adv. Twice, double; double-distilled, or strained, &c.; — a second time, again
s.m. A kind of pigeon; a paper kite; a species of eagle, the Nipāl hawk-eagle, Spizaëtus nipalensis
adj. (Geom.) Having two (opposite) sides equal (as an oblong)
(or du-) baćan, s.m. (Gram.) The dual number
dū-ba-dū, s.f. Facing, or confronting, of two people; — adv. Two together, two at a time; face to face, tête-¦-tête; in the presence (of, -kī), before
(-kī), To come face to face, to confront; to be confronted (with); — to quarrel, wrangle, to abuse each other (two people)
adj. & s.m. With two bullocks (a cart); — a two-bullock cart
(or do-) biswī, s.f. An allowance of two biswās out of twenty, ten per cent.
(-ke), 'To cause two words or "yeas" to be repeated'; to obtain the assent of two parties (to a contract); to get (a girl) married (in a quiet and inexpensive manner)
du-bhāsiyā, du-bhāshī, du-bhāshiya, s.m. lit. 'Knowing, or conversing in, two-tongues'; an interpreter
s.f. lit. 'Of two kinds'; double-dealing, duplicity
adj. & s.m. Two-faced, double-faced; — a double-faced man; a hypocrite
do-bheslī, du-bhesiyā, adj. Double-faced; doubtful, ambiguous, equivocal; vague; — s.m. A double-faced man ( = du-bhāṅtiyā)
s.m. pl. 'The two centres,' the foci (of an ellipse)
adj. Of two breadths or folds
adj. & s.m. Two-legged; — a biped; a man
(or do-) paṭṭā, s.m. lit. 'Having two breadths'; a kind of mantle, or a piece of linen, in which there are two breadths; a cloth or sheet thrown loosely over the shoulders, a wrapper
To sleep with the sheet drawn over the head; to sleep in peace or with a mind at ease; to sleep the sleep of death
To hold out, or wave, a flag of truce; to offer to surrender (a fort)
adj. Printed on both sides (a page)
s.f. A kind of cap consisting of two pieces; — du-palkā, s.m. lit. 'Having two eye-lids'; a kind of pigeon; — a kind of stone for a ring
adv. Before noon; ante meridiem, A.M.
v.n. The sun to decline from the meridian, to be past mid-day
adv. Past or after mid-day; post meridiem, P.M.
do-pahrī, adj. Of noon or mid-day; mid-day heat; — s.f. = do-pahar, q.v.
(do-) pahriyā, adj. & s.m. Of noon or noontide; meridian; — a kind of flower, Pentapetes phœnicea; — a child born at noon
(or do-) piyāza, s.m. A rich meat curry in which a double quantity of onions is used, and which has little or no gravy
adj. & s.m. Two-stringed; double-threaded; — a two-stringed instrument (as a guitar, &c.); a kind of shawl
du-tāh, adj. Two-plied, double; bent; — s.m. = du-tahī, q.v.
du-taʼī, s.f. Cloth that is lined; a piece of cloth having two breadths (used for lying upon)
adj. Two or three, a few
adj. Two-pieced; clear, plain, clean; decisive; pertinent
(-ko), To give a plain or decisive answer; to give a flat refusal or denial
v.t. To divide or cut in two, to sunder, to separate
v.n. To be divided or cut in two, to be sundered, be separated; to be at variance; — to be adjusted, be settled, to be completed
adj. & s.f. Pregnant; — a pregnant woman
do-jiyā honā, v.n. To be pregnant
adj. 'Two or four,' a few ( = do-tīn)
v.n. To meet, to have an interview
du-ćah, s.m. The second reservoir to which water is raised for the purpose of irrigation

(or do-) ćit or du-ćittā, adj. & s.m. Of two minds, divided in thought or sentiment, puzzled, distracted, doubtful, wavering, vacillating, fluctuating, irresolute; abstracted, absent-minded; — a waverer, &c.; — doubt, suspense, &c. ( = next, q.v.)
s.f. Doubt, suspense; wavering, vacillation; absence of mind, abstraction
s.m. A roof sloping two ways
du-ćandān, adj. Twice as much, as much again, double, two-fold
adj. & s.m. Having two poles; — a two-poled tent
s.m. A house with a double roof; a thatched roof sloping in two directions
adj. Having two compartments, &c.; of two stories ( = du-manzila)
s.m. A kind of ḥuqqa-snake with two bends (such as that of a guṛguṛī)
adj. & s.m. Having two naps (as cloth); — a kind of double-napped cloth
(fr. dām, 'thread'), s.f. Flowered muslin (the flowers being worked with a needle); — du-dasta, adj. (f. -ī), Two handed; having two handles; consisting of, or divided into, two branches or divisions
adj. lit. 'A two-days' guest'; temporary, transitory, fleeting, passing away (as beauty, &c.)
v.n. To die young, to die soon
s.m. A road dividing into two, or branching off in two directions; the junction of two roads

adj. (f. -ī), Double-faced ( = du-bhesiyā); having the same face or appearance on both sides, not having a wrong side; — having two faces or aspects, having the two sides of different colours (as cloth, &c.)
s.m. A soil consisting of clay and sand
s.m. A mixture of two kinds of things (as of tobacco, &c.)
du-rāsta, adj. Having a row of trees, or of houses, on both sides (a road)
adj. & s.m. Capricious, whimsical; — half-bred; — a mongrel, a person of double breed (having good qualities on one side, and bad on the other, so as not to be depended on; syn. doglā)
du-rangā, adj. (f. -ī), Of two colours; piebald; — capricious; double-dealing; hypocritical; — s.m. A capricious person; a double-dealer; a hypocrite
(or do-) rangī, s.f. The property of having two colours; capriciousness; double-dealing, duplicity, deceit, hypocrisy
adj. & s.m. Two-faced, double-faced, hypocritical; — a double-faced person, a hypocrite; — name of a flower (one face of which is yellow and the other red; syn. gul-ě-raʻnā)
adj. & s.m. Consisting of, or lasting for, two days; short, brief; the space of two days
s.f. The having two faces, double-facedness; duplicity, &c. ( = du-rangī, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Double-faced, &c. ( = do-rū); — adv. On both faces, or sides
(or do-) zānū baiṭhnā or ho-baiṭhnā, v.n. To kneel and sit on the heels behind; to sit on the hams or haunches
s.f. The being forked-tongued; the having two tongues, duplicity; equivocation
v.n. To pierce through and through, to go right through
adj. Of two years, two years old; biennial; — s.m. A two-year old colt; — lands that have been two years under cultivation
do-sāʼī, adj. & s.f. Yielding two crops a year (land; = du-fas̤lī)
s.f. The ploughing land twice; twice-ploughed land
s.m., or du-sūtī, s.f. A kind of cloth, the threads of which are double
s.m., or du-serī, s.f. A weight of two sers
adj. & s.m. Having two branches; forked (a branch); — the fork of a branch
s.m. A pair of shawls (worn like the du-paṭṭā); — du-shāle-posh, s.m. One who wears a du-shālā
adj. Double-bolted (as a lock); double-barrelled (as a gun); inflicting two blows, or a double wound
adj. (f. -ī), Having two sides, two-sided; on both sides, mutual, taking or pretending to take both sides in a dispute, deceitful
s.f. Double rule, the rule of two masters
adj. & s.f. Yielding two crops or harvests in the year (viz. the rabīʻ or spring harvest and the ḵẖarīf or autumnal harvest); — land yielding two crops in the year
v.t. To divide into two parts, to cut in two, to bisect
adj. Two-personed; fit for two persons
s.m. A double padlock, iron chains for the legs of criminals
adj. & s.m. Two-hunched; — a two-hunched camel, the Bactrian camel
(or do-) gāṛā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Twice, double; — a musket that carries two balls, a double-barrelled gun
adj. & s.m. Double, two together; of two sorts; — two genuflexions in prayer; a prayer in which two genuflexions are made; — two intimate friends, an inseparable pair
(or ḍo-) gun, du-gunā, adj. (f. -ī), Double, two-fold, twice as much

(or do-) ganḍī ćiṭṭī, s.f. A go-between, a fomenter of quarrels, one who flatters both parties in a dispute (the expression is taken from a game in which ćiṭṭī or tamarind seeds are used as dice, the seed being marked or rubbed on one face or side; and if it be fraudulently marked on both sides, it is called du-ganḍī)
s.m. Two vessels full of water placed one on top of the other (to pass a do-ghaṛ on the right hand is regarded as a good omen)
s.f. A space of two hours
adj. & s.m. Having two ropes; — a well with two lāʼos or ropes, and buckets
s.f. A wrap made of two kinds of stuff, one sewn upon the other; a quilted covering or robe, a double sheet
s.f. A kicking up, or a kick, with both hind legs (of a horse, or other quadruped)
du-lattī mārnā, To kick up or out with the two hind legs (a horse, &c.)

du-laṛā, and dolṛā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Having two strings or bands, of two rows; having two strings to one's bow; acting a double part, going from one party to another; — a necklace, or girdle, &c. of two strings or bands; — a piece of cloth consisting of two breadths
du-lohī, adj. & s.f. Composed of two plates of steel; — a scimitar made of two plates of steel joined together
du-maṭ, adj. & s.m. Consisting of two soils; — a mixture of two soils
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Brāhman class
du-manzila, adj. Of two floors or stories (a house, &c.); of two decks (a ship)
adj. & s.m. Having two mouths; two-faced, deceitful; — a two-faced or deceitful person; a kind of serpent with two mouths
v.n. To laugh and amuse oneself in moderation
(or do) nālī, vulg. do-nalī, adj. Double-barrelled (gun)
adj. Having two points or tips; (in Bot.) bifid
adj. & s.m. Divided in two, halved; — two halves
adj. & s.f. Consisting of two leaves; — a book of two leaves or pages, a small book
du-hatthar, adj. & s.f. Held with both hands; — a slap or blow with both hands at one time
s.f. A sword that is held with both hands
v.t. To strike a blow with a sword held with both hands
(-ko), To strike (one) with both hands at one time
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi दो (do), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑁄 (do), from Sanskrit द्व (dvá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dwáH, from Proto-Indo-European *dwóh₁.

def: s.f. Prayer, supplication (to God); an invocation of good, a blessing, benediction; wish; congratulation, salutation; imprecation of evil, curse, maiediction ( = bad-duʻā); — a verse, or verses, of the Qorʼān; — an incantation (pl. adʻiyat, or vulg. adʻiya)
subs: s.f. Prayer for the welfare (of anyone)
s.f. Prayer for the prosperity (of), benediction
(-ko), To give a blessing (to), to bless, to pray for
s.m. One who invokes a blessing, one who blesses or prays, a blesser; a well-wisher, friend
s.f. Invocation of a blessing, benediction, blessing
ex: It's your prayer (it's thanks to you); I'm fine, thanks
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُعَا (du'ā). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi دعا (d'a /⁠du'ā⁠/).

def: adj. Of two; — s.m. The two or deuce (at cards, dice, &c.).
s.m. See s.v. do.

ety: after the model of A., fr. دعائيّة, fem. of دعائی
def: adj. Benedictory; — s.f. Benediction, &c. ( = duʻā)
root: د ع ا ئ ي ي

def: s.f. A leather strap, a belt ( = duʼāl)
subs: s.m. lit. 'Having a leather belt,' a sipāhī, or sepoy.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀤𑀻𑀯𑀸𑀮𑀻 (dīvālī), 𑀤𑀻𑀯𑀸𑀯𑀮𑀻 (dīvāvalī) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from Sanskrit दीपावलि (dīpāvali), दीपावली (dīpāvalī). Doublet of دِیپاوَلِی (dīpāvalī).

def: s.f. See s.v. do.
s.f. Bent grass, panic grass, Panicum dactylon, or Cynodon dactylon (commonly used as fodder for horses, &c.).
s.m. Dip, dive, &c. ( = ḍubkī)
subs: v.n. To dip, dive ( = ḍubkī lagānā or mārnā).

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Sunk, submerged, immersed, under water; lost, &c. (see ḍūbnā); — s.m. One who is drowned, a drowned man; — flooded land, low land under water ( = ḍūbī zamīn)
subs: A ruined man, a bankrupt, insolvent
s.m. An island or bank left in the channel of a river; low land that is liable to be flooded
s.m. Lost expenditure, expenditure without return
s.m. Account or register of outlay or expenditure that is lost or yields no return, or of bad debts.

def: s.f. Drowning, &c.; — an unconditional bribe (given whether the donor gain or lose his suit, in contradistinction to tirānī or tarāʼī, a bribe which is to be returned if the suit be lost).

ety: dube, q.v.+āʼin = Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A woman of the dube caste; the wife of a dube.
s.f. = ḍubkī, q.v.
adj. Sinking (cf. ḍūbant)
subs: s.m. A sinking boat; a losing venture or speculation, whatever entails loss, or injury, &c.; a damning sin
'To prove a sinking ship,' to prove injurious or baneful, &c.; to fall into a damning sin.

ety: caus. of ḍūbnā; ḍubā˚ = ḍūb(nā)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to sink, to sink, dip, immerse, submerge, drown, flood; to lose, waste, exhaust, sacrifice, ruin, destroy, demolish
v.t. = ḍubānā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) Sunk, lost, irrecoverable, dead, bad (as a debt)
subs: s.f. A dead or bad bond

def: s.m. Drowning, sinking, immersion.
adj. & s.m. = dūbhar, q.v.
adj. = dur-bal, and dublā, qq.v.
adv. See s.v. do or du.
s.m. = ḍūbā or ḍūbā ćar, qq.v.

ety: fr. dūbar, and rt. of ghusirnā, with suff. ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Hiding or slinking away through weakness, helpless, insignificant, unknown; — a helpless, or an insignificant person, a nobody.
s.m. corr. of du-bhāsiya, q.v. s.v. do.

ety: ḍubā(nā)+āʼu or āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: adj. Enough to drown, deep, out of a man's depth.
s.m. See s.v. do.
s.m. State, condition; natural disposition, constitution, temper, quality, property; way, mode, manner, custom; — a bear; (met.) a fool
subs: or dubb-ě-aṣg̠ar, The constellation of the Lesser Bear
The constellation of the Greater Bear.

def: s.f. Diving, a dive ( = ḍubkī)
subs: s.m. One who dives, a diver

def: s.m. Doubt, suspense, uncertainty, perplexity, dilemma, indecision
subs: v.n. To be in a state of doubt, to doubt, to mistrust.

def: adj. & s.m. Being of two minds, undecided, wavering, in doubt, perplexed, puzzled, in a dilemma; — a waverer, &c.
s.m. Name of a class of Brāhmans who are (or are supposed to be) familiar with two of the four Vedas; a member of that class.
s.m. = dube, q.v.
s.m. A small kind of water-pot.
s.f. corr. of dur bhāgyatā, q.v.
s.f. Scarcity, dearth, famine, want.
adj. = du-bhāshī, q.v.s.v. do, 'two.'
adj. & s.m. Difficult to be borne, difficult, burdensome, troublesome, trying; — a burden, a trial, a troublesome person, an incubus.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. do, du, 'two.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. do, du, 'two.
s.f. A paste made of mahuʼā (q.v.).
s.m. Scarcity, famine.

ety: fr. dūb, q.v.+Prk. इओ, and इआ; S. इकः and इका
def: adj. Consisting of grass; — s.f. A kind of green, grass-green.
s.f. Dip, dive, plunge; sound made by a plunge (into water); gurgling
subs: (-ko), To dip, to immerse, submerge; to dive
v.n. To be dipped; — to dive ( = next)
ḍubkī mārnā, To dive, plunge, sink.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Lean, thin, meagre; poor, barren; weak, feeble.

ety: dublā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. = dublāpā, q.v.

ety: dublā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवें = S.आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To become lean, thin, or emaciated.

ety: dublā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Leanness, meagreness, thinness, emaciation; weakness.

ety: dublā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Leanness, meagreness, thinness, emaciation; weakness.

ety: dublā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Leanness, meagreness, thinness, emaciation; weakness.
v.n. = ḍūbnā, q.v.

ety: ḍūb˚, per met. = būḍ˚ = Prk. बुड्ड(इ) = S. व्रुड(ति), rt. व्रुड्
def: v.n. To dive; to sink, drown, be drowned; to drown oneself; to be immersed, be submerged, inundated, deluged, or flooded; to be lost, be sacrificed (as capital, reputation, &c.); to be destroyed, be ruined; to sink, go down, set (as the sun); to sink, faint (as the heart); to be absorbed, be engrossed, be lost (in business, or study, or thought, &c.)
subs: v.n. To be drowned, die by drowning; to drown oneself.

def: v.t. = ḍubonā, q.v.
v.t. = ḍubāwnā = ḍubānā, q.v.
adj. = dur-bal, and dublā, qq.v.

ety: fr. dūbar, and rt. of ghusirnā, with suff. ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Hiding or slinking away through weakness, helpless, insignificant, unknown; — a helpless, or an insignificant person, a nobody.
s.f. The percentage allowed to farmers on the revenue paid to government (formerly 'two in twenty,' or ten per cent.).

r: ḍubsī, ḍub˚, ḍūb, nā, sī, sā
def: s.f. Inundated land, land liable to be flooded.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Sinking, drowning, &c. (see ḍūbnā); — s.m. One who is drowning, a drowning man.

ety: v.n. fr. دبر 'to follow at the back'
def: s.f. The back; the hinder part (of a thing); the backside, posteriors, buttocks, the podex; — the latter part (of), the end, conclusion (as of a month; of prayers, &c.).
v.t. To cause to be sunk, or submerged, or drowned, &c., to have or get dipped, or sunk, &c. (see ḍūbnā, and ḍubānā).
s.m. See s.v. do.

ety: Pehl. dūt; Zend dunman, rt. du = S. धू; cf. S. धूम
def: s.m. Smoke, vapour, mist, haze, exhalation, breath
subs: s.m. 'Exhalation of the heart,' a sigh.

def: s.m. corr. of dūdh, q.v.
s.m. Family; — lamp-black; soot.

s.f. A sort of Asclepias, A. rosea; the plants Euphorbia hirta and E. thymifolia (the milk of which is commonly used by women to tattoo themselves); — chalk, a pencil of chalk; a kind of white stone (used in building); — breast or bubby.

ety: Prk. दुद्धं; S. दुग्धं, rt. दुह्
def: s.m. Milk; the milk or juice of certain plants; (fig.) family, race, caste, sect
subs: s.f. The ceremony of a bride and bridegroom eating a mess of boiled rice and milk and sugar together on the fourth day after marriage
v.n. The milk to descend (into the udder, or into the breasts of a woman), to have milk (in the ndder, or the breasts)
adj. & s.m. Living on milk; — one who lives on milk
(-meṅ), Milk to flow into and fill (the udder, or the breasts), to be full of milk (the udder, &c.) — to feel affection (for)
To sell milk; to sell (one's own, apnā) milk, to serve as a wet-nurse
(-meṅ), The milk to form (in plants, or in wheat, &c., before the grain)
adj. & s.f. Giving milk, suckling; — a wet-nurse; — dūdh-pūt, s.m. 'Milk and offspring'; milch-kine and children, cattle (or wealth) and children
adj. (f. -ī), Milk-drinking, sucking (as an infant, or the young of an animal); — interest-bearing, active (as money or capital); — dūdh ćhurānā (-kā), To wean ( = dūdh baṛhānā)
(-kā), To examine the milk (of a pregnant woman in order to ascertain from its thickness the sex of the unborn child)
adj. (f. -ī), Like milk, white as milk
s.m. A sucking calf, a sucking child

(aur) pānī-kā pānī karnā or kar-denā, 'To distinguish between what is really milk and what is pure water,' to sift the good or the true from the bad or the false; to exercise absolute and faultess justice
s.m. A child, or the young of an animal, that has not been suckled for the full period (owing to the mother's pregnancy, or other cause)
s.m. (f. -ā), The young of an animal
s.f. The double white rose-hay or oleander, Nerium coronarium
s.f. A milk-maid; a mother who suckles her child
'May you bathe in milk and bear many children,' may you be rich in cattle and children! — dūdh-hānḍī, or hanḍī, s.f. An earthen vessel for holding milk.

def: s.m. A species of rice.
adj. Giving milk, milch ( = dudhār).
adj. See ددهيل dudhail.
adj. = dudhal and dudhail, qq.v.
adj. See ددهار dudhār.
adj. Giving much milk; milk-giving, milch.
adj. Giving much milk; milk-giving, milch.
adj. Having milk, yielding milk, milky, milch; containing milky juice or sap (see dūdhiyā).
s.f. The breast (esp. of a mother or nursing woman); — starch; — name of several plants with milky juice (as various species of Asclepias, Echites, Euphorbia, &c.; see duddhī).

adj. Containing, or yielding, milk, &c. ( = dūdhī, q.v.); milk-white, white, pure; young, juicy, tender, green; — s.m. Name of several milky plants (see dūdhī and duddhī); — a young and tender head of maize or Indian corn, &c.
subs: s.m. A kind of white stone (used in building, and in making plaster, and utensils, &c.; see duddhī); — an opal.

def: s.m. A kind of disease in the rice-plant.

ety: mān = Pehl. mān = Zend nmāna, 'house,' &c., rt. man+ni = S. नि+मन्; cf. ḵẖān-o-mān, and māndan
def: s.m. An illustrious house, a noble family, a great tribe; house, family, race, generation.
adj. Two-fold, double, twice as much or as many ( = do-guṇā); — dugan, s.m. The second tone in music.
adj. & s.m. = dogāṛā, q.v.
adj. Milked; — s.m. Milk ( = dūdh)
subs: s.m. The froth or skim of milk; cream; syllabub
s.f. Name of a small medicinal shrub
s.m. The froth of milk, syllabub; milk and mangoes, mango-foule

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of inferior Brāhmans on the borders of Allahabad.
s.f. Milkiness, milky nature.
adj. (f. -inī), Having milk, milky, milch.
s.f. A species of Asclepias, A. rosea ( = dūdhī).
adj. = dugdhī, q.v.
s.f. A red species of the Achyranthes aspera.
v.n. To be beaten, to sound (as a drum or ḍugḍugī, q.v.).
s.f. = dhugdhugī, q.v.
s.f. A small kettle-drum (used in making proclamations, and by jugglers, &c.); proclamation by drum
subs: To play the ḍugḍug̠ī; to proclaim by beat of drum ( = ḍugḍugī pīṭnā)
v.n. The drum to be sounded, proclamation to be made by drum
To proclaim by drum, to make public.

def: s.f. = ḍugḍugī, q.v.
s.m. A sling-basket of large size, round and deep, used for irrigation.
adj. Two-fold, double, twice as much or as many ( = do-guṇā); — dugan, s.m. The second tone in music.
adj. & s.m. Imparting, yielding; — yielder, &c. (occurring only as last member of Hindī compounds).
intj. Help! mercy! justice! (often used in obsecration, e.g. Akbar-kī duhāʼī hai, 'I appeal to, or swear by, Akbar'; — bhagwān-kī duhāʼī, 'By God!' God help us! — rām-duhāʼī, By Rām (I swear)
subs: To cry repeatedly for justice, or help; to make reiterated complaints
dukāʼī karnā (-kī), To cry for help, or mercy, or justice.

def: s.f. = دوهائي dohāʼī, q.v.
s.m. A widower who marries again (cf. dojhā and dūj-bar).
s.m. Milking, the act of milking.
v.t. To cause to be milked, to have or get milked.
s.f. = dohnī, q.v.
s.m. A milker ( = dohne-wālā, dohiyā).
v.n. To be doubled, to be folded up.
adj. (f. -ī), Hard, difficult, arduous, weighty, burdensome.
s.f. (dialec.), Desire, inclination, lust.
s.f. Mound, mud pillar, boundary mark; alluvial formations.
s.f. Daughter
subs: or duhitr̤i-pati, s.m. Daughter's husband, son-in-law.

def: s.f. Daughter
subs: or duhitr̤i-pati, s.m. Daughter's husband, son-in-law.

ety: Ap. Prk. दूहिअउ, Prk. दूविहइओ; S. द्वि+विध+इकः
def: s.m. A two-faced or double ćūlhā, or fire-place.
s.m. Oil; ointment, unguent (see adhān).

ety: duh˚ = S. दुह्य(ते), pass. fr. rt. दुह्; but, as a trans. verb, prob. = dohnā
def: v.t. To milk; — v.n. To be milked.
s.f. A milk-pail ( = dohnī, q.v.).

ety: for A. دهنية, fr. duhn, q.v.
def: s.f. Fatness, oiliness, unctuosity; oil, ointment ( = duhn; syn. ćiknāʼī).
adj. = dohrā, q.v.
v.t. = dohrānā, q.v.
s.m. = dohrāʼo, q.v.
adj. Doubled, &c. ( = dohrā, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. dus.
adj. (dialec.) = donoṅ, q.v.
s.m. A drum, a tabor.
adj. (Braj) = donoṅ, q.v.
adj. (Braj) = donoṅ, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), That may be milked, requiring to be milked, milkable.
s.f. A milch animal.

ety: Prk. दुए or दुवे; S. द्वि
def: adj. (dialec.), Two ( = do).
s.f. The being two or two-fold, duality; matching, pairing; a second, another
adj. & s.m. (old H.) = dvij, q.v.
s.f. The second day of a lunar fortnight
adj. = dūjā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Second, other, another; secondary; — s.m. The second one; the second spirit or soul.
s.m. (dialec.) = dūjā, q.v.
s.m. A man who marries a second wife, a bigamist (cf. dojhā).
s.m. A spindle.
s.m. A blow with the fist
subs: or ḍukkam-ḍukkā, s.m. Mutual fisticuffing, boxing.

def: s.m. (dialec.) 'Bad season'; scarcity, famine.
s.f. A shop ( = dūkān, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Sweepings of a (druggist's) shop; compound medicine.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُکَان (dukān), borrowed from Arabic دُكَّان (dukkān), borrowed from Aramaic דּוּכָּנָא.

def: s.f. A shop, workshop; a business
subs: (-kī), To remove the shop or business (of); to mar or ruin the business (of)
dūkān band karnā, To close a shop
v.n. A shop or business to flourish
s.f. 'Shop-keeping words'; falsehood, deception, trickery, humbug
To keep a shop; to open a shop
To set up or to open a shop or business; to arrange goods for sale, to display (one's) wares.

def: s.f. Shop-keeping; — s.m. A shop-keeper.
s.f. An old or decrepit woman
subs: s.m. A tradition or custom of, or relating to, old women; an old woman's tale.

def: s.f. A daughter ( = duḵẖtar, q.v.); — an aquatic plant or reed (of which mats are made).

s.m. Pain, ache, ailment, affliction, suffering, distress; misery, trouble, sorrow, grief, uneasiness, unhappiness; difficulty; oppression; vexation, annoyance, bother; fatigue, labour, toil; — adj. (f. -ā), Painful, disagreeable, unpleasant; uneasy, uncomfortable, troublesome, difficult
subs: v.n. To suffer pain, to be afflicted; to experience difficulty, &c.; to take trouble or pains
(-kā), To share the trouble or sorrow (of another)
dukh bhugatnā, v.n. To suffer distress or trouble, put oneself to incovenience; to labour, toil
adj. (f. -ā), Full of trouble, suffering excessively, abounding with distress or trouble, &c.
s.m. A breaking or destroying of grief or affliction; an epithet of God (considered as the destroyer of affliction)
dukh-bhogī, adj. & s.m. Suffering or sharing pain, sorrow, or trouble; — one whose portion is pain, &c.
v.n. To suffer pain or trouble, to be afflicted
adj. & s.m. Pain-imparting; — a giver or causer of pain, &c.
s.m. One who passes life in pain or distress
part. Pierced by sorrow, pained, afflicted, distressed

dukh-dānī, dukh-dāyak, dukh-dāʼī or dāyī, adj. & s.m. Pain-imparting, painful, afflictive, grievous, troublesome, vexatious, oppressive; — one who gives or occasions pain, a painer, tormentor, persecutor, &c.
adj. & s.m. Burning up affliction; — one who burns up or destroys the miseries, &c. (of another)
adj. Burnt or tormented by affliction, pained, distressed
s.m. Trouble and toil, hard or distressing work
part. adj. (poet.) = dukh-diyā, perf. part. of next, q.v.
(-ko), To give pain (to); to occasion trouble, to bother
s.m. 'Ocean of trouble,' the world; deep distress
dukh-kārī, dukh-kar, dukh-kartā, adj. & s.m. Causing or occasioning pain, sorrow, or trouble, &c.; — one who occasions pain, &c. (see dukh-dāʼī)
adj. & s.m. Afflicted, wretched, miserable; — afflicted one, poor wretch
adj. & s.m. Pain-destroying; — one who destroys the trouble, &c. (of another)
adj. & s.m. Grief-removing, &c.; — dukh-labhya, adj. To be effected or obtained with difficulty, hardly procurable
adj. & s.m. Abounding in pain, consisting of suffering, &c.; — one whose nature is made up of suffering
s.m. The uprooting of grief or trouble, &c.
adj. & s.m. Preventing or warding off trouble, or affliction, &c.; — the prevention of trouble, &c.
dukh-hartā, dukh-haraṇ, adj. & s.m. Taking away pain or grief, &c.; — a remover or reliever of pain, &c.; — an anodyne
adj. & s.m. Destroying or removing pain, trouble, or sorrow, &c.; — pain-destroyer, pain-killer.

def: adv. In a painful or cutting manner, &c.
s.m. Smoke; steam; fume (of tobccco, &c.).
v.t. To inflict pain, to pain, to hurt; to torment, to grieve; to touch or probe so as to hurt (a sore, &c.).
adj. Of or appertaining to smoke, or steam; smoky; steamy; steam

ety: Prk. दुक्खालओ and दुक्खालइओ; S. दुःख+आल+कः and इकः
def: adj. & s.m. = dukhiyārā, dukhī, dukhiya, qq.v.

ety: Prk. दुक्खालओ and दुक्खालइओ; S. दुःख+आल+कः and इकः
def: adj. & s.m. = dukhiyārā, dukhī, dukhiya, qq.v.

ety: Prk. दुक्खिअओ; S. दुःखित + कः; — dukhiyārā = dukhī+Prk. आलओ = S. आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Pained, afflicted, in pain or distress, distressed, suffering, ill, sick; affected, grieved, sorrowful, sad; unfortunate, poor, indigent, miserable, wretched; — afflicted one, sufferer, distressed person, unfortunate one, poor wretch.
adj. = dukhī, q.v.
part. adj. Pained, suffering pain, distressed, afflicted, tried, grieved
subs: v.n. To be ever suffering, to be distressed, &c.

ety: Prk. दुक्खिअओ; S. दुःखित + कः; — dukhiyārā = dukhī+Prk. आलओ = S. आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Pained, afflicted, in pain or distress, distressed, suffering, ill, sick; affected, grieved, sorrowful, sad; unfortunate, poor, indigent, miserable, wretched; — afflicted one, sufferer, distressed person, unfortunate one, poor wretch.

ety: Prk. दुक्खिअओ; S. दुःखित + कः; — dukhiyārā = dukhī+Prk. आलओ = S. आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Pained, afflicted, in pain or distress, distressed, suffering, ill, sick; affected, grieved, sorrowful, sad; unfortunate, poor, indigent, miserable, wretched; — afflicted one, sufferer, distressed person, unfortunate one, poor wretch.

ety: dukh˚ = Prk. दुक्ख(इ) or दुक्खे(इ) = S. दुःखय(ति), fr. दुःख
def: v.n. To pain, ache, smart, throb; to feel pity or compassion (for); to feel compunction or remorse.
s.m. Great, or constant, suffering; distress, calamity, misfortune, woe; hard labour, toil
subs: v.n. To be afflicted, or distressed, &c., to be overtaken by misfortune
v.n. To live a life of toil and trouble
(apnā), To bewail (one's) woes or troubles, to tell (one's) misery, &c.
(apnā), To go about telling (one's) grievances or troubles.

ety: Pehl. dukht; Zend dughdhar; S. duhitar
def: s.f. Daughter, girl; a virgin, maid
subs: duḵẖtar-ě-tāk, or duḵẖtar-ě-raz, s.f. 'Daughter of the vine,' wine; a grape

ety: Pehl. dukht; Zend dughdhar; S. duhitar
def: s.f. Daughter, girl; a virgin, maid
subs: duḵẖtar-ě-tāk, or duḵẖtar-ě-raz, s.f. 'Daughter of the vine,' wine; a grape
wety: PIE word *dʰugh₂tḗr Borrowed from Classical Persian دُخْتَر (duxtar, “daughter”).

def: s.f. Little daughter; little girl.
s.m. Entering, entrance, admission, &c. (see daḵẖl); penetrating, penetration; pudenda feminœ; — income, revenue, &c.
v.t. To give (one) a blow, to thump, beat (with the fist).
s.m. (f. -ī), A hog, boar (syn. sūʼar).
s.m. The fourth part of a paisā.
adj. Worth one-fourth part of a paisā.
s.f. Two of anything, a pair; — a snaffle.
s.m. A species of plant; woven silk, very fine cloth or raiment (made of the inner bark of this plant).
s.m. = dulhā, q.v.
s.f. See s.v. do, du.
s.f. See s.v. do.
s.f. = dhulaiṅḍī, q.v.

ety: dulakh˚ = Prk. दुलक्क(ई) or दुलक्के(इ), fr. S. दोल (rt. दुल्)+कृ
def: v.t. To swerve from, to disobey, to kick against; to decline, refuse.
s.m. (local) Nonentity, non-existence ( = nāṭh).

ety: dola, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To shake, agitate; to toss.
v.t. To move, shake, swing, agitate
s.m. Shaking, agitating, agitation, &c.
s.m. Fondness, love, affection
adj. (f. -ī), Beloved, dear, darling.
v.t. To fondle, to show affection to, to love, to pet.
s.m. A turban; a sash; the fine cloth of which a turban is made.

ety: v.n. fr. دلدل 'to put in motion'
def: s.m. A hedgehog; — a certain mule belonging to the prophet Moḥammad (which he gave to his son-in-law ʻAlī); — adj. Fat; flabby
subs: 'Rider of duldul,' a title of ʻAlī.

def: s.f. The dolphin (supposed to save one who is drowning).
s.f. The dolphin (supposed to save one who is drowning).
s.m. A bridegroom.
s.m. = dulhā, q.v.
s.f. = dhulenḍī, q.v.

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).
s.f. = dulhan, q.v.

ety: S. दुलि; Prk.-S. डुलि; prob. fr. S. दुल्
def: s.f. A small turtle; a female turtle.
s.m. = dulīćā, q.v.

ety: prob. corr. fr. g̠ālīća = qālīća, vulg. galīćā
def: s.m. A carpet.

ety: Prk. - S. डुलिका; prob. S. दुल्+इका
def: s.f. A kind of wagtail.

ety: Prk. दुलक्किआ, fr. S. दुल or दोल+कृ; cf. dulakhnā
def: s.f. Trot (of a horse, or dog); — a dog-trot
subs: dulkī ćalnā, v.n. To go at a trot, to trot.

def: s.m. = dulhā, q.v.
s.m. A venomous animal like a spider, a tarantula.
s.m. A purse or bag (worn or carried on the waist), a letter-bag.
v.n. To move, stir, &c. ( = ḍolnā, q.v.); — to walk with a peculiar twisting of the body (a gait much affected by women; — cf. ḍagmagānā).

ety: Pehl. dum; Zend duma, rt. du = S. धू
def: s.f. Tail; end, extremity; — a constant follower (see next)
subs: s.m. Tail of a paper kite; a tail, generally; (met.) one who is ever at the heels of another (as a child that follows its mother about), a constant follower
adv. With the tail between the legs
v.n. To put the tail between the legs, to drop the tail, to turn tail, to run away ( = dum dabā-kar bhāgnā)
adj. In good condition, plump, fat
v.n. 'To make the tail erect'; to run away with the tail stuck out (as an elephant, &c.)
s.m. The root, or place of growth, of the tail (of a bird, or other animal), the coccyx
s.m. The wagging of the tail (as of a dog, when pleased)
To wag the tail ( = dum hilānā)
(-kī), To creep under the tail (of, as pups under a bitch); to take refuge or protection (with); to dangle or run (after), to follow about, to cling (to, as a child to its mother's skirt)
To shake or wag the tail (as a dog, &c.).

def: s.m. A leather case in which tea is imported into Garhwāl and Kamāʼon.
s.m. Water of the eye (arising from disease, or old age); water that flows from the grape-vine in spring.

ety: S. डोम+pleon. suff. ār = Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: s.m. = ḍom, q.v.
s.m.f.(?), The glomerous fig-tree, Ficus glomerata.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. do.
s.f. Tail, &c. ( = dum, q.v.).
s.m. A kind of sheep with a large, round, and fat tail; — adj. (met.) Fat.
s.m. See دنبال dumbāl.
s.m. A boil, a swelling, a felon, an imposthume, an abscess, a bubo ( = dummal, q.v.).

r: dumbāl, dumbālā, dumbāla, dumb, āl, ār
def: s.m. A tail; end or extremity (of anything); the outer corner (of the eye); the shank (of a sword, or dagger, &c.); the rudder (of a ship); hinder part or rear (of a saddle, &c.); — adv. After, behind, in pursuit ( = pīćhe)
subs: 'A star having a tail,' a comet.

r: dumbālā, dumbāl, dumbāla, dumb, āl, ār
def: s.m. A tail; end or extremity (of anything); the outer corner (of the eye); the shank (of a sword, or dagger, &c.); the rudder (of a ship); hinder part or rear (of a saddle, &c.); — adv. After, behind, in pursuit ( = pīćhe)
subs: 'A star having a tail,' a comet.

r: dumbāla, dumbāl, dumbālā, dumb, āl, ār
def: s.m. A tail; end or extremity (of anything); the outer corner (of the eye); the shank (of a sword, or dagger, &c.); the rudder (of a ship); hinder part or rear (of a saddle, &c.); — adv. After, behind, in pursuit ( = pīćhe)
subs: 'A star having a tail,' a comet.

def: s.f. A crupper; the lower part of the back
subs: s.f. The crupper bone, the os sacrum, the coccyx.

def: s.m. A boil, &c., see dumbal.
s.f. A small boil or swelling.
adj. (in comp. = dūnā), Double, &c.; see dūnā
subs: (-se), To boast, to brag, to be in the habit of bragging (before, or to).

def: A tract of country at the foot of hills, a valley.
adj. Low, base, vile, ignoble, grovelling, mean, paltry, poor, inferior, contemptible
adj. (f. -ī), Two-fold, double, twice as much, twice, multiplied by two ( = dugnā)
subs: dūnā-dūnā, adj. & adv. Twice as much again; more and more, all the more; — s.m. The double of a thing added thereto.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa दूण (dūṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀉𑀡 (duuṇa), 𑀤𑀺𑀉𑀡 (diuṇa), from Sanskrit द्विगुण (dviguṇa).

def: adj. contrac. of dūnā, q.v.
s.m. A large kettle-drum ( = dundubhī); — noise, din, tumult, uproar, riot; outrage, injustice ( = andher)
subs: To make a noise, create an uproar; to perpetrate an outrage, to practise oppression.

def: s.m. = ḍunḍu, ḍunḍubh, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.), A dust-cloud, a whirlwind.
s.m. A bullock with one horn, or with broken or crumpled horns.
s.m. = dund, dundu, dundubhī.
s.m. A sugar-mill; the outlet for the smoke at a sugar-mill ( = dhundkā).
s.m. = dundubhi, q.v.
s.m. = ḍunḍubh, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ā), A kind of lizard without feet, Amphisbæna; a kind of snake.
s.m. A large kettle-drum; — an epithet of Krishṇa; — an epithet of an Asura, and of a Rākshas; — dundubhī, s.f. A kettle-drum; — a pair.
s.m. A sort of red worm.
adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound.

ety: ḍoṅgā+ān = Prk. अणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ: S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.m. Depth, deepness; abyss, gulf.
s.f. (local), A small fractional division of an estate.

ety: Prk. डोंगरो; perhaps fr. S. उदग्रः; cf. ḍāṅg
def: s.m. Hill, mountain; hilly country; high ground.
adj. = ḍūṅgā, q.v.
s.f. A coarse kind of cloth.

ety: prob. S. धानक+कः; see dāna, in comp. with which it is com. used
def: s.m. Crumb, scrap, pickings.

ety: fem. of ادني adnā, 'nearer,' &c.; rt. دنو 'to be or become near'
def: s.f. (lit. 'Nearer, nearest, former'); the present world, the present life or state of existence; the people of this world, people; a whole world, a multitude; — worldly enjoyments or blessings, worldly goods, the good things of this life, wealth, riches
subs: adj. & s.m. Worldly; worldly-minded; — a person of the world; one absorbed in worldly affairs, a mammonist, one possessed of the good things of this world, a rich man
s.f. Worldliness, attention to the concerns of the world; worldly affairs; economy; mammon, worldly goods or possessions, riches; wife and children, family; — a show of politeness
s.f. Seeking the goods of the world, worldliness
The world and whatever is in it.

def: adj. Belonging to the world, worldly, secular.
adj. Belonging to the world, worldly, secular.
